title: 5.3 The Breathing System
description: Quiz covering the human breathing system: structure, mechanism of breathing, gas exchange, lung capacities, and respiratory diseases.

questions.question: What is the primary function of the breathing system in the human body?
options: Remove waste products like carbon dioxide
Provide oxygen to body tissues for cellular respiration
Break down food for nutrient absorption
questions.correctAnswer: Provide oxygen to body tissues for cellular respiration

questions.question: Through which structures does air enter the human breathing system?
options: The nasal cavities or the mouth
questions.correctAnswer: The nasal cavities or the mouth

questions.question: What is the function of the tiny hairs that line the nasal cavities?
options: They produce mucus to trap bacteria
They act as a filter, keeping foreign particles out of the lower breathing tract
questions.correctAnswer: They act as a filter, keeping foreign particles out of the lower breathing tract

questions.question: What role does mucus play in the nasal cavity?
options: It traps foreign particles and keeps cells moist
It produces oxygen for the blood
It filters carbon dioxide from the air
questions.correctAnswer: It traps foreign particles and keeps cells moist

questions.question: Which systems does the pharynx serve?
questions.correctAnswer: The breathing and digestive systems

questions.question: Which structure connects the pharynx to the trachea?
questions.correctAnswer: The larynx

questions.question: What does the epiglottis cover during swallowing to prevent food from entering?
questions.correctAnswer: The opening of the trachea

questions.question: What is the glottis made up of?
options: The arytenoid cartilage and vocal ligament
The vestibular folds, true vocal cords, and the space between them
questions.correctAnswer: The vestibular folds, true vocal cords, and the space between them

questions.question: How many stacked pieces of cartilage form the trachea?
What is the function of the cartilage rings in the trachea?
options: To provide structural support and prevent the trachea from collapsing
To produce mucus for trapping particles
To contract and push air into the lungs
To exchange gases with the blood
questions.correctAnswer: To provide structural support and prevent the trachea from collapsing

questions.question: What structures branch directly from the trachea toward the lungs?
options: The right and left primary bronchi
questions.correctAnswer: The right and left primary bronchi

questions.question: What is the approximate diameter of a bronchiole?
questions.correctAnswer: 1 mm

questions.question: Approximately how many terminal bronchioles are found in each lung?
questions.correctAnswer: More than 1000

questions.question: Where does actual gas exchange take place in the breathing system?
questions.correctAnswer: In the alveoli

questions.question: What is an alveolar sac?
options: A single air sac that exchanges gases
A tube that carries air to the alveoli
A cluster of many individual alveoli
A cartilage structure in the lung
questions.correctAnswer: A cluster of many individual alveoli

questions.question: Alveoli have elastic walls that stretch during air intake. What is the benefit of this?
options: It greatly increases surface area available for gas exchange
It decreases the pressure in the chest cavity
It prevents mucus from entering the alveoli
questions.correctAnswer: It greatly increases surface area available for gas exchange

questions.question: What is the function of alveolar pores that connect alveoli to their neighbours?
options: To allow blood to flow between alveoli
To transport mucus out of the alveoli
To increase the number of alveoli
To help maintain equal air pressure throughout the alveoli and lung
questions.correctAnswer: To help maintain equal air pressure throughout the alveoli and lung

questions.question: What percentage of atmospheric air is oxygen?
What percentage of exhaled air is carbon dioxide?
According to the principles of gas movement in breathing, gases move from an area of:
questions.correctAnswer: High pressure to low pressure

questions.question: When does inspiration (inhalation) occur?
options: When the pressure inside the lungs is less than atmospheric pressure
When the pressure inside the lungs is greater than atmospheric pressure
When the pressure inside the lungs equals atmospheric pressure
questions.correctAnswer: When the pressure inside the lungs is less than atmospheric pressure

questions.question: When does expiration (exhalation) occur?
options: When the pressure inside the lungs is greater than atmospheric pressure
questions.correctAnswer: When the pressure inside the lungs is greater than atmospheric pressure

questions.question: During inspiration, what does the diaphragm muscle do?
questions.correctAnswer: It contracts and pulls downward

questions.question: During expiration, what happens to the diaphragm?
options: It relaxes and returns to its dome shape
It moves upward and compresses the lungs
questions.correctAnswer: It relaxes and returns to its dome shape

questions.question: During inspiration, what action do the external intercostal muscles perform?
options: They relax, allowing the ribs to fall
They contract, pulling the ribs down and in
They contract, pulling the ribs up and out
questions.correctAnswer: They contract, pulling the ribs up and out

questions.question: During expiration, what action do the internal intercostal muscles perform?
options: They contract, pulling the ribs down and in
They contract, pulling the ribs up and out
questions.correctAnswer: They contract, pulling the ribs down and in

questions.question: In bronchitis, what happens to the bronchial tubes?
questions.correctAnswer: The mucous lining becomes inflamed

questions.question: What happens to the alveolar walls in emphysema?
options: They become irritated, lose flexibility, stretch, and eventually rupture
questions.correctAnswer: They become irritated, lose flexibility, stretch, and eventually rupture

questions.question: What is the most common cause of emphysema?
questions.correctAnswer: Smoking

questions.question: Bronchial asthma is linked to inflammation of which structures?
questions.correctAnswer: The bronchioles
